Off Calendar Apr-28-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
John Edison Rosenthal of GOLD LAW FIRM (415-986-1339, X-141), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Larry Hightower and Shirley Hightower

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS LARRY HIGHTOWER & SHIRLEY HIGHTOWER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF DEFENDANT ALCATEL LUCENT USA, INC.'S AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE NOS. ONE THROUGH FORTY TWO [CODE OF CIV. PROC. 437C(F)(1)]

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for summary adjudication of defendant Alcatel Lucent USA, Inc.'s affirmative defenses nos. one through forty-two is granted. Plaintiffs' motion will be considered because they gave proper notice pursuant to this Court's order specially setting a trial date, which did not limit the agreed-upon summary motion briefing schedule to motions brought by defendants. Defendant's request to deny the motion, or at least to continue it, pursuant to C.C.P. 437c(h) is denied for failure to meet the requirements of section 437c(h). As those were defendant's only arguments opposing the motion, the motion is substantively unopposed and thus granted in its entirety. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-28-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
John Edison Rosenthal of GOLD LAW FIRM (415-986-1339, X-141), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Larry Hightower and Shirley Hightower

RULING:
DEFENDANT ALCATEL LUCENT USA, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No other appearnace and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Alcatel-Lucent USA, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs' action against it is barred by Georgia's Workers' Compensation scheme. However, even if defendant had sustained its burden, plaintiffs' Exhibits A through K would create a triable issue whether plaintiffs' action is barred by Georgia's Workers' Compensation scheme. If the Court were to get to plaintiffs' evidence, the Court would not consider Mr. Garofolo's declaration due to relevance. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Southwire Company, Llc For Stay Based Upon Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Apr-02-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The following tentative ruling was posted on March 30, 2015 and there was no notice given to the Court that any part was contesting it, therefore the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant Southwire Company, LLC's motion to stay the action on the grounds of forum non conveniens is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that California is a seriously inconvenient forum as the balance of private and public interest factors makes it just that the litigation remain in California. Although not required when seeking solely a stay rather than a dismissal, the Court will point out that defendant did not demonstrate that Georgia is a suitable alternative forum because it did not show that all defendants are subject to jurisdiction there. (See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744; American Cemwood Corp. v. American Home Assurance Co. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 431.) A proposed order previously submitted by plaintiffs' counsel was signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
